title: Writing - Part 1: Social Inequality
description: Your class has been discussing the causes of social inequality and what governments and individuals can do to create a fairer society.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the causes in your notes. You should explain which cause you consider most significant, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Children from disadvantaged backgrounds rarely receive the same quality of education as those from wealthy families.
The gap between the richest and poorest members of society has widened dramatically in recent decades.
People face barriers based on gender, race or background that prevent them from reaching their potential.

structure.intro: Introduce social inequality and state the two causes you will discuss.
structure.body1: Discuss the first cause and how it contributes to inequality.
structure.body2: Discuss the second cause and compare its significance to the first.
structure.conclusion: State which cause you regard as most significant and suggest what could be done.

content.modelAnswer:
Social inequality remains one of the most persistent challenges facing modern democracies. Despite economic growth in many countries, the gap between the most and least privileged members of society continues to widen. Two of its most significant causes are unequal access to education and the growing gap between income and wealth.

Education is one of the most powerful factors determining a person's life chances. When the quality of schooling depends largely on whether a family can afford to live in a particular area or pay private school fees, children from less privileged backgrounds start life at a serious disadvantage. This inequality in education reproduces and deepens inequality across generations.

The widening income gap further entrenches social divisions. When a small number of individuals and corporations accumulate vast wealth while wages for ordinary workers stagnate, social mobility becomes increasingly unlikely. Economic inequality also leads to unequal access to healthcare, housing and political influence.

In my view, unequal access to education is the most significant cause. It is the mechanism through which disadvantage is passed from one generation to the next. By investing in high-quality public education for all children, regardless of background, societies can begin to break this cycle and offer genuinely equal opportunities to every citizen.
